items.title: Dow surpasses 53K; sets new closing record after holiday weekend
items.content: Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at record 53,056 on July 6. S&P 500 added 0.7%, Nasdaq 100 rose 1.3%. Strength in blue-chips and semiconductors drove gains, though chip/tech weakness had weighed on Nasdaq previously.

items.title: Dow Closes at Record After Crossing 53000 for First Time
items.content: Dow Jones crossed 53,000 for the first time and closed at a record high on July 2. Comcast stock surged on plan to split. S&P 500 and Nasdaq rebounded after tech sell-off.

items.title: Hang Seng rocketed 3.97% as market closes on July 6
items.content: Hong Kong's Hang Seng surged 3.97% (+900.24 pts) to close at 23,572.10 on July 6. Intraday high 23,686.56. Index up 2.37% over past five sessions but remains 16% below 52-week high.

items.title: Hang Seng Extends Rally on Biotech and Internet Stock Strength
items.content: Hong Kong benchmark extended outperformance vs regional peers. Investors continued rotation into HK stocks. Biotech and internet names led the rally.

items.title: HK stocks tumbled during first-half global rally
items.content: Hang Seng was among worst-performing major benchmarks in H1 2026. Investors missed AI semiconductor boom and suffered from concerns about China economy. IPO juggernaut tipped to continue in H2.

items.title: Measurement tweaks will make inflation data look better
items.content: Core PCE inflation at 3.4% in May, above Fed's 2% target every month since March 2021. Measurement methodology changes expected to make future data look more favorable.

items.title: May Spending Rebounds, but Inflation Takes Its Toll
items.content: Core PCE prices up 3.4% from year ago. Given recent decline in oil prices, TCB projects inflation likely peaked in Q2 2026.

items.title: US and Iran agree to continue discussions, host Qatar says
items.content: US and Iran reached initial agreement to open Strait of Hormuz and extend ceasefire. Oil and gas could reach global market. Separate meetings in Qatar; discussions ongoing.

items.title: Samsung, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads
items.content: SMIC Hong Kong-listed shares fell more than 11%. Hua Hong Grace also tumbled. Chip selloff spread from Wall Street to Asia. Semiconductor sector under pressure.

items.title: SSE Composite Slips Marginally as AI Rotation Continues
items.content: SSE Composite around 4,041 on July 6, reflecting substantial recovery from mid-2025 lows. Index gained over 16% YoY. AI rotation continues to drive sector dynamics.

items.title: Veteran Investor: China's Full Stack AI Is Overlooked and Underpriced
items.content: Analyst Billy Leung on Bloomberg argued global investors underweight China AI economy. Wave of fundraising and HK listings signals repricing. Kling $2B deal cited as catalyst.

items.title: Earnings Calendar for Week of July 6-10
items.content: PepsiCo earnings expected with $2.18 EPS forecast, weak PFNA segment expected. Mon Jul 6: 21 earnings, Tue Jul 7: 5, Wed Jul 8: 7, Thu Jul 9: 13, Fri Jul 10: 1.
